subject: Human Resources
title: Geographic Pay Differentials
description: A quick, practical guide to Geographic Pay Differentials.

hook.text: A remote employee in Boise does the same work as a colleague in San Francisco—who earns 40% more. Fair?

buildup.text: Geographic differentials adjust pay based on cost of living—higher in expensive cities, lower in affordable areas.

discovery.text: This was straightforward when people worked in offices. Remote work blew up the model entirely.

twist.text: Should pay follow the person or the role? Companies disagree fiercely—and employees notice every difference.

climax.text: Some companies now use location tiers or national rates. There's no perfect answer—only trade-offs in fairness and cost.

punchline.text: Same work, different zip code. The pay question has no easy answer.

quiz.question: What challenge did remote work create for geographic pay?
options: It's unclear if pay should follow the person or the role's location
Remote workers don't need to be paid
All locations have the same cost of living
